abstraction-with-base-interfaces.label: Abstraction With Base Interfaces
abstraction-with-base-interfaces.summary: Use a simple base class/interface idea to define what every object must provide.

try_abstraction_with_base_interfaces_ske.title: Implement a Base Class Method
try_abstraction_with_base_interfaces_ske.prompt: Create a base class `Appliance` with a method `turn_on()`. Implement two subclasses, `WashingMachine` and `Refrigerator`, each with its own `turn_on()` method.

try_abstraction_with_base_interfaces_ske.prompt: Create a base class `Animal` with a method `speak()`. Implement two subclasses, `Dog` and `Cat`, each with its own `speak()` method. Demonstrate polymorphic behavior by iterating over a list of `Animal` objects and calling `speak()`.

fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.title: Base Class Method Requirement
fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.prompt: In a base class, a method that must be implemented by subclasses is often defined with a placeholder. What is this placeholder?
fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.hint: It's a common exception used to indicate that a method should be overridden.

help.concept: Base classes use a placeholder to indicate methods that must be implemented by subclasses.
help.hint_1: This placeholder is an exception that signals unimplemented methods.
help.hint_2: The exception is raised to enforce method implementation in subclasses.

fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.template: In a base class, a method that must be implemented by subclasses is defined with [blank1].

fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-1.title: Polymorphism in Action
fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-1.prompt: Polymorphism allows methods to be called on objects of different types through a common [blank1].
fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-1.hint: This term refers to a shared method signature across different classes.

help.concept: Polymorphism relies on a shared method signature to operate on different object types.
help.hint_1: The term describes a shared method signature in a base class.
help.hint_2: This shared method signature allows polymorphic behavior.

fill-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-1.template: Polymorphism allows methods to be called on objects of different types through a common [blank1].

single-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-.title: Purpose of Base Classes
single-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a base class in object-oriented programming?
single-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-.hint: Think about how base classes enforce a common structure.

help.concept: Base classes define a common structure that subclasses must follow.
help.hint_1: Base classes ensure that all subclasses implement certain methods.
help.hint_2: They provide a template for subclasses to follow.

options.a: To provide a common interface for subclasses

single-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-.title: Method Overriding
single-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-.prompt: Why do subclasses override methods from a base class?
single-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-.hint: Consider how subclasses might need to change behavior.

help.concept: Subclasses override methods to provide specific behavior.
help.hint_1: Overriding allows subclasses to change the method's behavior.
help.hint_2: It enables customization of inherited methods.

options.a: To provide specific behavior for the subclass
options.b: To remove methods from the base class

multi-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.title: Characteristics of Polymorphism
multi-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.prompt: Which of the following are characteristics of polymorphism in object-oriented programming? Select all that apply.
multi-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

options.a: Allows objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass
options.b: Enables method calls on different object types through a common interface
options.c: Requires all classes to have the same attributes
options.d: Forces all subclasses to have the same methods

drag-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.title: Order of Method Implementation
drag-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.prompt: Arrange the steps for implementing a method in a subclass that overrides a base class method.
drag-abstraction-with-base-interfaces-0.hint: Start with defining the subclass and end with implementing the method.

help.concept: Overriding a method involves defining the subclass and implementing the method.

tokens.t4: Call the superclass method if needed

abstraction-with-base-interfaces-sketch0.title: Understanding Base Interfaces
abstraction-with-base-interfaces-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: In object-oriented programming, a base interface defines a set of methods that derived classes must implement. This ensures that all subclasses adhere to a common contract, allowing for polymorphic behavior. For example, consider a base class `Shape` with a method `area()`. Any subclass like `Circle` or `Rectangle` must implement the `area()` method, ensuring that you can calculate the area regardless of the specific shape type.

try-it-yourself.bodyMarkdown: Try it yourself: change one value in the example, predict the new output, then run the code to check your prediction.
